refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a persistent, auditable baseline, and provide managed updates with traceability to source worksite inputs and calibration events.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked specificity on persistence, auditability, and traceability—critical for safety-critical systems. Refinement adds explicit mention of safety margins (high safety criticality), baseline persistence, and traceability linkage to worksite inputs and calibration. This ensures the requirement is verifiable and testable.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (2) baseline records include timestamp, source inputs, and derivation method; (3) updates are logged with reason (calibration, policy change, safety event); (4) traceability chain from worksite input → derived target → acceptance test is documented and auditable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept worksite mission context inputs (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, safety constraints) and map them to performance requirement templates.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall compute and record performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) with derivation method and timestamp.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall maintain a persistent, versioned baseline of performance requirements with full audit trail (who, when, why, source inputs).
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall provide managed baseline updates triggered by calibration events, policy changes, or safety events, with rollback capability.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall expose performance baselines and update history via documented interface for acceptance testing and compliance reporting.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Baseline Engine—a dedicated service that ingests worksite mission context, applies deterministic derivation rules (lookup tables, algorithms), computes targets, persists to a versioned baseline store, and exposes via REST/gRPC API.

DP-B: Distributed Performance Derivation—embed derivation logic in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; each computes targets locally and synchronizes to a shared baseline store via eventual consistency.
DP-C: Manual Performance Specification—Site Manager manually enters performance targets via UI; system records and versions them without automatic derivation.
DP-D: Hybrid Baseline Engine—centralized derivation service for initial baseline; distributed edge modules for real-time performance monitoring and local threshold updates; periodic reconciliation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Baseline Engine with deterministic derivation rules, persistent versioned baseline store, and documented API exposure.

potential_sources: Coupling to worksite mission context input format (must be standardized across all stakeholders).
Coupling to acceptance test framework (must consume baseline API).
Coupling to Safety Services (safety margins must be validated by safety module).
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling to Vehicle and Teleoperation internals (they consume baselines, not vice versa). Coupling is primarily to external inputs (worksite context) and to Safety Services for validation. This is acceptable and manageable via interface contracts.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON schema) before system deployment.
ASSUMPTION: Derivation rules (lookup tables, algorithms) are stable and can be codified; they do not require frequent ad-hoc changes.
RISK: If derivation rules are domain-expert-dependent and change frequently, DP-A becomes brittle. Mitigation: establish rule versioning and expert review process.
RISK: Centralized baseline store becomes a single point of failure. Mitigation: implement replication, backup, and recovery procedures.
RISK: High safety criticality requires that baseline derivation be validated and certified. Mitigation: implement formal validation and traceability to safety case.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend adding explicit requirement that derivation rules be formally validated against safety case and that baseline updates trigger safety re-certification if safety margins change.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the Performance Baseline Engine, including derivation logic, baseline persistence, versioning, audit trail, and API exposure. It is the system-level integration point for performance requirement management.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, safety constraints) from Site Manager or external mission planning system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exposes current performance baseline, baseline history, and derivation metadata to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for real-time monitoring and acceptance testing.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety event notifications (e.g., near-miss, anomaly) that may trigger baseline review; sends safety margin updates to Safety Services for validation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates baseline updates and audit logs to remote systems (e.g., cloud backend, compliance reporting) for distributed traceability.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration point and owns the overall performance requirement management lifecycle. Baseline Engine is a core system service. Minimal diffusion: only API exposure to Vehicle and Teleoperation.

Vehicle module is a consumer of performance baselines, not an owner. Embedding derivation logic in Vehicle would scatter the single source of truth and create coupling between Vehicle and worksite mission context. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation module is a consumer of performance baselines for remote monitoring. Embedding derivation logic would couple Teleoperation to worksite context and create inconsistency with Vehicle-side baselines. High diffusion risk.

Safety Services validates safety margins and may trigger baseline reviews, but does not own derivation logic. Containment score reflects validation responsibility, but derivation is a system-level concern, not a safety-specific concern.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Baseline Engine is a system-level service that must be owned by NGHE Solution to ensure single source of truth, consistent derivation across all consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), and auditable traceability. Containment in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and coupling.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module is too large or poorly structured, the Baseline Engine may become difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: implement Baseline Engine as a well-defined sub-component with clear interfaces.

RISK: Baseline updates must be synchronized across Vehicle and Teleoperation in real-time. If network latency is high, Vehicle and Teleoperation may operate on stale baselines. Mitigation: implement caching and eventual consistency with conflict resolution.

RISK: Safety Services must validate baseline updates before they take effect. If validation is slow, system responsiveness may suffer. Mitigation: implement asynchronous validation with rollback capability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign IM to NGHE Solution. Recommend that Baseline Engine be implemented as a dedicated microservice or module within NGHE Solution with clear API boundaries (Tasking in, Telemetry out, SafetyAlert inout, Network Link inout). Implement caching and eventual consistency for Vehicle and Teleoperation consumers.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Baseline Engine) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) accepts worksite mission context via Tasking interface, (2) computes and records targets with derivation method, (3) maintains persistent versioned baseline, (4) manages updates with audit trail, (5) exposes via API. No functional gaps. Coupling is acceptable and manageable.

diffusion_risks: Baseline Engine must be accessible to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules via Telemetry interface. If interface contract is not well-defined, consumers may implement inconsistent caching or fallback logic. Mitigation: formalize Telemetry interface specification and versioning.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment is appropriate. Baseline Engine is a system-level service, not a Vehicle or Teleoperation concern. Containment score of 95 reflects strong fit. Diffusion score of 5 reflects minimal risk of logic leakage to other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via: (1) unit tests of derivation rules against test cases, (2) integration tests of Tasking input → baseline output, (3) audit trail inspection for traceability, (4) acceptance tests that consume Telemetry interface. All verification points are within NGHE Solution scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns Baseline Engine; Vehicle and Teleoperation are consumers. Interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined. Ownership and responsibility boundaries are explicit.

recommended_revisions: Formalize Telemetry interface specification to include baseline schema, versioning, and caching semantics.
Define SafetyAlert interface contract for baseline review triggers and safety margin validation.
Establish baseline update approval workflow (e.g., Site Manager approval, Safety Services validation) to ensure traceability and compliance.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 has been refined into five atomic sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5). Each sub-FR is now specific, testable, and traceable. DP-A is sound and well-justified. IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate with low diffusion risk. All gates pass. The next level of decomposition should address each sub-FR individually to derive detailed DPs and IMs for worksite context input handling, derivation rule implementation, baseline persistence, update management, and API exposure.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept worksite mission context inputs (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, safety constraints) and map them to performance requirement templates.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall compute and record performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) with derivation method and timestamp.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall maintain a persistent, versioned baseline of performance requirements with full audit trail (who, when, why, source inputs).
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall provide managed baseline updates triggered by calibration events, policy changes, or safety events, with rollback capability.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall expose performance baselines and update history via documented interface for acceptance testing and compliance reporting.

assumptions: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured, machine-readable format before system deployment.
Derivation rules are stable and can be codified; they do not require frequent ad-hoc changes.
Safety Services module is available and capable of validating safety margins.
Network Link interface supports reliable, low-latency communication for baseline synchronization.
Site Manager and Tele-operator have access to baseline history and audit trail for compliance reporting.
open_risks: Derivation rules may be domain-expert-dependent and change frequently, making DP-A brittle. Mitigation: establish rule versioning and expert review process.
Centralized baseline store is a single point of failure. Mitigation: implement replication, backup, and recovery procedures.
High safety criticality requires formal validation of baseline derivation. Mitigation: implement formal validation and traceability to safety case.
Baseline updates must be synchronized across Vehicle and Teleoperation in real-time. Mitigation: implement caching and eventual consistency with conflict resolution.
Safety Services validation may introduce latency in baseline updates. Mitigation: implement asynchronous validation with rollback capability.
evidence_needed: Worksite mission context schema and example inputs from Site Manager.
Derivation rules (lookup tables, algorithms) from domain experts.
Safety case and safety margin requirements from Safety Manager.
Baseline persistence and versioning requirements from Maintenance/Quality.
API specification and interface contracts for Vehicle and Teleoperation consumers.
Audit trail and compliance reporting requirements from Project Manager.
